    1. UnicodeSymbols()
  7. Methodendetails

    3. getSafeSymbol(String, String)

Klasse UnicodeSymbols

java.lang.Object
ch.framedev.lagersystem.utils.UnicodeSymbols
public class UnicodeSymbols extends Object
Cross-platform symbols for consistent display on Windows, macOS, and Linux. Uses Unicode escape sequences to keep the source ASCII-friendly.

  • Methodenübersicht

    Modifikator und Typ
    Methode
    Beschreibung
    static String
    getSafeSymbol(String emoji, String fallback)
    Legacy method for backward compatibility.
    static String
    getSymbol(String emoji, String fallback)
    Gets a symbol that will display on the current platform.
    static String
    safeSymbol(String symbol, String fallback, Font font)
    Gets a safe symbol with font-specific rendering support.

    Von Klasse geerbte Methoden Object

    clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

      public static final String COL_MINDESTBESTELLWERT

  • Konstruktordetails

    • UnicodeSymbols

      public UnicodeSymbols()

  • Methodendetails

    • getSymbol

      public static String getSymbol(String emoji, String fallback)
      Gets a symbol that will display on the current platform. Uses emoji on Windows and macOS when font support is available, falls back to ASCII text on other platforms or when emoji unavailable.
      Parameter:
      emoji - the primary emoji/symbol to use (Windows/macOS/Linux)
      fallback - ASCII text fallback (unsupported platforms)
      Gibt zurück:
      emoji if supported, otherwise fallback text

    • safeSymbol

      public static String safeSymbol(String symbol, String fallback, Font font)
      Gets a safe symbol with font-specific rendering support. This method checks if the given font can display the symbol. Use this for UI elements where you control the font directly.
      Parameter:
      symbol - the emoji/symbol to use
      fallback - ASCII text fallback (used on Windows if font doesn't support)
      font - the Font to check for symbol support (can be null)
      Gibt zurück:
      symbol if supported by font, otherwise fallback text

    • getSafeSymbol

      public static String getSafeSymbol(String emoji, String fallback)
      Legacy method for backward compatibility. Now delegates to getSymbol for consistent behavior.
      Parameter:
      emoji - the primary emoji/symbol to use
      fallback - ASCII text fallback
      Gibt zurück:
      safe symbol or fallback
